channel.title: TRIMARC Incident and Construction Feed
channel.description: Know Before You Go - Visit https://www.trimarc.org

item.title: 294731 : I-71/75 North-South between MM 190.0 and 191.1 Approaching Brent Spence Bridge in Kenton County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : COVINGTON, Ky. (June 22, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight lane closures on Interstate 71/75 in Kenton County beginning on Monday, June 22. Crews will be filling in rumble strips and performing striping operations ahead of an upcoming traffic shift. Crews will be working on I-71/75 northbound and southbound from south of the 12th Street/MLK Jr. Boulevard off-ramps (190 mile point) to the southern approach of the Brent Spence Bridge (191.1 mile point), a distance of 1.1 miles. Beginning at 8 p.m., crews will block a single left lane in the project area. A double left lane closure will begin at 11 p.m. All lanes are expected to reopen by 5 a.m. daily. All exit ramps currently open will remain open during this portion of the project. Motorists are encouraged to find alternate routes utilizing I-275 and I-471 during working hours to avoid delays. No work will be permitted to take place during the Fourth of July holiday weekend (Friday, July 3 through Sunday, July 5). Weather permitting, this work is expected to be completed by Friday, July 17. KYTC will provide an update once the traffic shift is scheduled.

item.title: 292105 : I-65 South between MM 136.0 and 133.0 Between Jefferson Street and University Blvd. in Jefferson County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : I-65 North from MM 130 to MM 136 in Jefferson County is closed for repairs through July 31, 2026 I-65 South from MM 136 to MM 133 in Jefferson County is closed for repairs through July 31, 2026 I-65 South ramps from University Boulevard, Eastern Parkway and Crittenden Drive have reopened. Detour via I-264 East/West. For downtown access, take the Jefferson Street exit from I-65 South. For additional information, please visit https://i65centralcorridor.com/.

item.title: 292104 : I-65 North between MM 130.0 and 136.0 Between I-264 and Muhammad Ali Boulevard in Jefferson County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : I-65 North from MM 130 to MM 136 in Jefferson County is closed for repairs through July 31, 2026 I-65 South from MM 136 to MM 133 in Jefferson County is closed for repairs through July 31, 2026 I-65 South ramps from University Boulevard, Eastern Parkway and Crittenden Drive have reopened. Detour via I-264 East/West. For downtown access, take the Jefferson Street exit from I-65 South. For additional information, please visit https://i65centralcorridor.com/.

item.title: 298040 : I-71 North between MM 1.5 and 2.5 Area of Zorn Avenue in Jefferson County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight traffic impacts on Interstate 71 North beginning Monday, July 20, as part of the $50 million I-71 widening and improvement project in Louisville [links-1.govdelivery.com]. To safely facilitate bridge construction, I-71 North will be reduced to one lane each night from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., with brief rolling closures occurring between midnight and 4 a.m., on the following dates: Beginning at 8 p.m. on Monday, July 20, through 5 a.m. on Tuesday, July 21 Beginning at 8 p.m. on Tuesday, July 21, through 5 a.m. on Wednesday, July 22 Beginning at 8 p.m. on Wednesday, July 22, through 5 a.m. on Thursday, July 23 During the overnight rolling closures (between midnight and 4 a.m.), motorists traveling on I-71 North will be diverted to exit at Exit 2 (Zorn Avenue) and immediately re-enter the interstate using the I-71 North on-ramp. This short, signed detour will be used while traffic is temporarily stopped to allow crews to safely set bridge beams between midnight and 4 a.m. To help minimize delays, traffic signals at the I-71/Zorn Avenue interchange will be placed in flashing mode, and flaggers will be stationed at all approaches to efficiently direct motorists through the interchange. Additionally, brief closures of Zorn Avenue are possible to allow crews to safely move and set bridge beams. Motorists should reduce speed, follow posted signage and flagger directions, and allow extra travel time during the scheduled overnight work.

item.title: 298053 : I-275 East between MM 82.0 and 79.5 Between Turkeyfoot Road and Madison Pike in Kenton County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of evening and overnight lane closures on Interstate 275 in Kenton County beginning Tuesday, July 21. Crews will be performing concrete repairs on several bridge decks. Crews will be blocking the left two lanes on I-275 eastbound from Turkeyfoot Road (KY 1303) (82 mile marker) to east of the bridge over Madison Pike (KY 17) (79.53 mile point), a distance of 2.47 miles. Lane closures will begin at 7 p.m. All lanes will reopen by 6 a.m. daily. All ramps are expected to remain open during this project. Motorists should anticipate some delays and are encouraged to find alternate routes. Weather permitting, this maintenance work is expected to be completed Friday, July 24 at 6 a.m. KYTC will advise if additional nights of work are needed. This project is part of a series of projects focused on concrete repairs to improve conditions on our area's interstate bridges. KYTC will advise when work shifts to other local bridge overpasses. Project schedules are subject to change due to inclement weather or other unforeseen circumstances. For the most current information on road construction in Northern Kentucky, please visit the District 6 Roadshow [links-1.govdelivery.com] or the Driving Northern Kentucky Progress [links-1.govdelivery.com] website. You can also stay informed by following KYTC District 6 on Facebook [links-1.govdelivery.com] and X [links-1.govdelivery.com] (formerly Twitter). For statewide traffic and travel updates, visit GoKY [links-1.govdelivery.com].

item.title: 297186 : I-275 East-West between MM 12.9 and 13.8 Area of Carroll Lee Cropper Bridge in Boone County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : The Non-Redundant Steel Tension Member (NSTM) inspection is a close-up, hands-on examination of the bridge’s critical members. The most recent routine inspection on the Carroll Cropper Memorial Bridge took place July 2025. The NSTM inspection will require single lane closures on the four-lane bridge from Monday, July 13, through Friday, July 24. Motorists should expect intermittent single lane closures throughout the inspection period outside of the lane closure restriction times listed below. The lane closure restriction times have been put in place to mitigate traffic delays during peak travel times. Lane closure restrictions: I-275 eastbound: No closures Monday through Thursday from 3 p.m. to 6 p.m. Additionally, Friday from 2 p.m. to 7 p.m. I-275 westbound: No closures Monday through Friday from 6 a.m. to 8 a.m. Additionally, Friday from 3 p.m. to 7 p.m. The inspection is being performed by AECOM. Weather permitting, the overall project has an expected completion date of Friday, July 24. I-275 Carroll Cropper Bridge –​ 13.56 mile point – A routine bridge inspection is underway. This work will require daily single lane closures: I-275 Eastbound: 6 a.m. to 3 p.m. I-275 Westbound: 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. Weather permitting, this inspection is expected to be completed by Friday, July 24. I-275 – 12.985 mile point – Overnight rolling roadblocks on a portion of I-275 to install new overhead utility wires spanning I-275 west of Exit 11 (to Petersburg) is complete. U.S. 42 – 0.0 – 5.67 mile points –​ A resurfacing project on U.S. 42 between the Boone/Gallatin County line and Richwood Road is in progress. Milling and paving operations began Tuesday, July 14. ​​Motorists should expect single lane closures throughout the project area. When Boone County Schools are in session, this work will take place between 9 a.m. and 3 p.m. There are no time restrictions for work when school is out for spring and summer break. Weather permitting, this work is expected to be completed late Summer 2026.

item.title: 254857 : I-264 East-West between MM 21.5 and 22.9 Area of US-42 in Jefferson County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : UPDATE (March 23, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight lane and ramp closures on Interstate 264 East and West (Watterson Expressway) beginning Tuesday, March 24, as part of the $130 million widening and improvement project of I-264 and U.S. 42 (Brownsboro Road) [links-1.govdelivery.com]. Beginning Tuesday, March 24, at 8 p.m., through Wednesday, March 25, at 6 a.m.: Alternating lane closures will occur on I-264 East and West between mile markers 23 and 22 (from the I-264/I-71 split to U.S. 42/Brownsboro Road). Beginning Wednesday, March 25, at 8 p.m., through Thursday, March 26, at 6 a.m.: The I-264 East ramp to I-71 South will be closed. Motorists should continue on I-264 East, take I-71 North, then follow I-265 North to reconnect with I-71 South. Alternating lane closures will occur on I-264 East between mile markers 23 and 22 (from the I-264/I-71 split to U.S. 42/Brownsboro Road). Motorists should heed signage, use caution when traveling through the work zone, and plan ahead for travel delays. The highway improvement project is expected to be completed by the Fall of 2027. More details, including project timelines and traffic impacts [links-1.govdelivery.com], will be shared online as work progresses. Motorists are encouraged to sign up for email or text alerts [links-1.govdelivery.com] regarding weekly traffic impacts pertaining to the project.

item.title: 269220 : I-71 North-South between MM 0.1 and 2.0 Between I-64/I-71 Interchange and Zorn Ave. in Jefferson County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : The I-71 widening and improvement project is a $50 million infrastructure investment to enhance safety and improve mobility along one of Louisville's most critical travel corridors. The two-year project, focused on increasing traffic capacity and safety, will enhance regional connectivity, provide reliable travel for years to come, and support economic growth. The project spans approximately two miles of interstate from the I-65/I-71 interchange near Frankfort Avenue to the I-71/Zorn Avenue interchange. Additional improvements include the construction of a multi-use path along Zorn Avenue between the I-71/Zorn Avenue interchange and Mellwood Avenue, as well as a new median treatment at the intersection of Zorn Avenue and Mellwood Avenue to enhance safety and better define traffic movements. The project also includes the demolition and reconstruction of the I-71 bridge over Beargrass Creek, along with a latex overlay on the I-71 bridge over I-64 at mile point 0.3 to extend the structure’s service life. For additional information, please visist https://transportation.ky.gov/DistrictFive/Pages/I-71-Widening---Louisville-.aspx.

item.title: 288246 : I-65 South Ramp to St. Catherine St in Jefferson County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : The ramp from I-65 South to St. Catherine (Exit 135) will be closed until further notice.

item.description: Construction / Road Work : Ramp from KY-61 to I-65 North is closed. For additional information, please visit https://i65centralcorridor.com/

item.title: 292980 : I-71/75 North Ramp to 12th Street/MLK Jr. Boulevard in Kenton County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : (June 5, 2026) – Interstate 71/75 ramp closures and detours in Covington are needed to safely advance construction on the Brent Spence Bridge Corridor Project, which will add interstate lanes, better access, a new companion bridge and convert the existing bridge for local travel to improve safety and increase regional highway capacity. Temporary closures and detours will now begin on Monday, June 8, and will take place in multiple phases. On Monday, June 8, the following closures are expected to take place: I-71/75 northbound off-ramp to 12th Street/MLK Jr. Blvd. (0.0 - 0.28 mile points): this closure will remain in place through Summer 2027. Detour: Traffic will use the I-71/75 northbound 5th Street exit ramp, turn left (north) onto Philadelphia Street, then left onto 4th Street driving west to the I-71/75 southbound ramp to Pike Street. Turn left on to Pike Street, then right on to Simon Kenton Way, then left on to MLK Blvd. KYTC will provide further updates ahead of the closures to inform all road users via the Brent Spence Bridge Corridor project website. [links-1.govdelivery.com] Project schedules are subject to change due to inclement weather or other unforeseen circumstances. For the most current information on road construction in Northern Kentucky, please visit the District 6 Roadshow [links-1.govdelivery.com] or the Driving Northern Kentucky Progress [links-1.govdelivery.com] website. You can also stay informed by following KYTC District 6 on Facebook [links-1.govdelivery.com] and X [links-1.govdelivery.com] (formerly Twitter). For statewide traffic and travel updates, visit GoKY [links-1.govdelivery.com].

item.title: 292981 : I-71/75 South Ramp from 12th Street/MLK Jr. Boulevard in Kenton County
item.description: Construction / Road Work : (June 5, 2026) – Interstate 71/75 ramp closures and detours in Covington are needed to safely advance construction on the Brent Spence Bridge Corridor Project, which will add interstate lanes, better access, a new companion bridge and convert the existing bridge for local travel to improve safety and increase regional highway capacity. Temporary closures and detours will now begin on Monday, June 8, and will take place in multiple phases. On Monday, June 8, the following closures are expected to take place: I-71/75 southbound on-ramp from 12th Street/MLK Jr. Blvd. (0.1 - 0.3 mile points): this closure will remain in place through October 2026. Detour: Traffic will use Pike St/Dixie Highway (U.S. 25) to Kyles Lane (KY 1072), to I-71/75 southbound. The I-71/75 southbound off-ramp to Fifth Street (KY 8) will remain open while W. Third Street in Covington is blocked to thru traffic. Once W. Third Street reopens, the southbound W. Fifth Street off-ramp, as well as Fifth Street from Crescent Avenue to the I-71/75 northbound exit off-ramp west of Philadelphia Street (6.5 - 6.67 mile points) will close. An updated timeline for the reopening of Covington's W. Third Street and closure of the I-71/75 southbound off-ramp to W. Fifth Street will be provided at a later date. KYTC will provide further updates ahead of the closures to inform all road users via the Brent Spence Bridge Corridor project website. [links-1.govdelivery.com] Project schedules are subject to change due to inclement weather or other unforeseen circumstances. For the most current information on road construction in Northern Kentucky, please visit the District 6 Roadshow [links-1.govdelivery.com] or the Driving Northern Kentucky Progress [links-1.govdelivery.com] website. You can also stay informed by following KYTC District 6 on Facebook [links-1.govdelivery.com] and X [links-1.govdelivery.com] (formerly Twitter). For statewide traffic and travel updates, visit GoKY [links-1.govdelivery.com].
